LAW AND MOTION, 302, MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER DENYING MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION DOES NOT COMPLY WITH CCP 1008(A). DEFENDANT DOES NOT PROVIDE A DECLARATION IN COMPLIANCE WITH THE STATUTE. DEFENDANT FAILS TO HIGHLIGHT ANY NEW FACTS, CIRCUMSTANCES, OR LAW. LASTLY, DEFENDANT FAILS TO EXPLAIN WHY HIS "NEW" EVIDENCE COULD NOT HAVE BEEN PRESENTED EARLIER. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR #5963; DEPT. 302 (302/EHG)

PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO SUBMIT TO THE CONTEST E-MAIL ADDRESS OF THE COURT WITH A COPY TO MS. LAGUNA ONLY. calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Nov-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John G. Warner, Esq. 415-924-2640 for Plaintiff.
John Spencer, Esq. 415-308-6469 of Spencer Law Firm for Defendant Alina Laguna.

RULING - A R G U E D. Parties report status of several tax motions. Defendant argues entitled to compensation. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT ALINA LAGUNA's Motion To Tax Costs.

Taken Under Submission.*** Plaintiff submits a proposed order.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

***After the hearing, the Court signs an Order granting in part and denying in part Defendant Alina G. Laguna's moion to tax costs. (see scanned order for details)***

Notice Of Motion To Tax
Off Calendar Apr-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JOHN G. WARNER, ESQ. of Law Offices of John G. Warner (Tel: 415-924-2640) appearing for the plaintiff/judgment creditor King Security Services, Inc.;
Atty. JOHN S. SPENCER, ESQ. of Spencer Law Firm (Tel: 415-308-6469) appearing for defendant/judgment debtor Alina G. Laguna.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear. The Court notes that today's hearing is a continuance from a prior hearing to permit Mr. Warner to present argument. The Court notes that the tentative ruling posted in advance of that prior hearing remains as the tentative ruling to be argued today.

The matter is argued by counsel for parties and submitted.

The Court modifies its prior tentative ruling and rules as follows:

Judgment debtor Alina Laguna's motion to tax costs is granted in part and denied in part. The Court allows the plaintiff $2,000.00 out of the attorney fees claimed in the memorandum of costs after judgment filed by judgment creditor King Security Services, Inc. The Court taxes the remainder of the attorney fees claimed by the plaintiff. King Security is entitled to the $835.90 in other costs claimed on that memorandum. Ms. Laguna's request to tax the fees claimed in the prior memorandum of costs after judgment is untimely and thus denied.

The Court instructs plaintiff to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR #6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion To Tax
Off Calendar Apr-02-2018 Continued To Apr-05-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
John S. Spencer, Esq. of Spencer Law Firm, T:415.308.6469, for defendant/moving party;
John G. Warner, Esq. of the Law Offices of John G. Warner, T:415.924.2640, for plaintiff/opposing party.

The parties did not contest the Court's tentative ruling, which was adopted.

DEFENDANT ALINA LAGUNA'S Motion To Tax is CONTINUED to April 5, 2018 to be heard by Judge Kahn.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.

Notice Of Motion To Tax

9:36 am.

10:06 am.
RULING - ARGUED. The matter is recalled. The Court vacates the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ALINA LAGUNA's Motion To Tax Costs is continued to 4/2/18 at 9:30 am. Plaintiff to give notice to the Defendant. Plaintiff's counsel to advise the Court of any conflict.
(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

Notice Of Motion To Tax
Off Calendar Feb-15-2018 Continued To Feb-15-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ALINA LAGUNA's Motion To Tax.

Judgment debtor Alina Laguna's motion to tax costs* is granted in substantial part. All attorney fees claimed in the memorandum of costs after judgment filed by judgment creditor King Security Services, Inc. are taxed as not recoverable per the combination of CCP 685.040 and Civil Code 1717.5 and in the court's discretion because the total fees already included in the judgment are greatly disproportionate to the non-fees part of the judgment. King Security is entitled to the $835.90 in other costs claimed on that memorandum. Ms. Laguna's request to tax the fees claimed in the prior memorandum of costs after judgment is untimely and thus denied.

Ms. Laguna is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.*

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

*Before 5:00 pm, Off the record, Judge Kahn, informs the clerks Plaintiff's Counsel John Warner will be appearing tomorrow 2/15/18 (per email to the Court) to contest the tentative ruling. The matter is continued to 2/15/18*

MINI MINUTES:

NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Alina Laguna's motion to set aside default and default judgment is denied. The motion is untimely per CCP 473.5(a). Ms. Laguna was served with a notice of the entry of judgment on February 10, 2016. (Warner Dec. Ex. K.) Ms. Laguna filed this motion on December 6, 2016 and therefore failed to bring this motion within the 180 day time period required by section 473.5(a). Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to submit to the contest e-mail address of the court with a copy to Ms. Laguna only. OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar May-27-2015 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Motion To Set Aside Default And And Default Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-14-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JAMES SOWERS' Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Motion To Set Aside Default And And Default Judgment is denied. Defendant's motion does not comply with CCP 1008(a). Defendant does not provide a declaration in compliance with the statute. Defendant fails to highlight any new facts, circumstances, or law. Lastly, defendant fails to explain why his "new" evidence could not have been presented earlier. "To be entitled to reconsideration, a party should show that (1) evidence of new or different facts exist, and (2) the party has a satisfactory explanation for failing to produce such evidence at an earlier time." Kalivas v. Barry Controls Corp., (1996) 49 Cal.App.4th 1152, 1160-1161; see also Gilberd v. AC Transit, (1995) 32 Cal.App.4th 1494, 1500. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-09-2015 Continued To May-27-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Feb-06-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JOHN G. WARNER, Tel No. (415) 924-2640 appearing for Plaintiff King Security Services, Inc.;
Defendant JAMES SOWERS, Tel No. (415) 234-3786 appearing in pro per.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JAMES SOWERS' MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND ANY DEFAULT JUDGMENT is denied. Plaintiff demonstrates that defendant was properly served. Actual physical delivery of process is not necessary when a defendant is being recalcitrant. Cases recognize the sufficiency of attempted delivery in the presence of a defendant who refuses to accept the papers. See In re Ball, (1934) 2 Cal.App.2d 578, 579; Trujillo v. Trujillo, (1945) 71 Cal.App.2d 257, 260. Defendant fails to demonstrate that the default was entered as the result of mistake, surprise, inadvertence, or excusable neglect. Defendant ignored the papers. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
